baseline.prompt_text: Cinematic fantasy, rainy ancient temple courtyard, wet stone floor. One continuous medium shot at eye level with a static camera. The black armored warrior delivers one brief controlled forearm impact. Show visible upper-torso contact before the white haired warrior's torso yields. The resulting weight shift causes exactly one grounded half-step backward, then the receiver stabilizes. Preserve both identities and natural foot contact. No reaction before contact, repeated hits, multiple steps, prolonged slide, flight, fall, wall collision, extra subject, magic, injury, gore, camera movement, or scene change.

candidate.prompt_text: Initial State: Cinematic fantasy in a rainy ancient temple courtyard on a wet stone floor. In one continuous medium shot at eye level with a static camera, the black armored warrior and the white haired warrior are balanced at contact range with both feet planted. Physical Event: The black armored warrior delivers one brief controlled forearm impact to the receiver's upper torso; visible contact occurs before any receiver motion and ends immediately after the impulse. Body Mechanics: The contact compresses the receiver's torso, moves the center of mass backward over the rear foot, unloads the front foot, and causes exactly one grounded half-step backward; the feet remain in contact with the floor and there is no continued slide. Final State: The receiver stabilizes after the single half-step in a balanced stance, with the attacker no longer applying force and both identities, spacing, scene, and camera preserved. No sustained push, reaction before contact, repeated hit, multiple steps, sliding, flight, fall, wall collision, body merge, extra subject, magic, injury, gore, camera movement, or scene change.

comparison.isolated_variable: Prompt organization and explicit state-transition wording only.

hypothesis: A four-block state-transition prompt will preserve a brief readable impact while improving impulse transfer, rear-foot loading, one bounded half-step, and stable recovery relative to the V3 causal prompt.
purpose: Compare the existing V3 causal-motion treatment with a V4 state-transition treatment for impact step-back.
success_criteria: Both characters begin balanced and grounded at readable contact range.
One visible forearm-to-upper-torso contact precedes all receiver motion.
The event remains a brief impulse rather than sustained pressure.
Torso compression and center-of-mass movement precede rear-foot loading.
Exactly one grounded half-step follows without continued sliding or flight.
The attacker stops applying force and the receiver reaches a stable final stance.
Identity, limb ownership, scene, framing, and camera remain fixed.
